flow_diagnostics.dominant_signal: SAS controls glass and second-chance math; Wembanyama is materially altering OKC shot quality and possession outcomes.
flow_diagnostics.OKC_regression_signal: SGA has not activated as scorer. OKC still has live comeback equity because the deficit is only 7 despite SGA at 4 points and OKC 27.3% from three.
flow_diagnostics.SAS_risk: No Fox increases late-half and late-game ball security risk. Castle has 5 turnovers and OKC pressure can flip game state fast.
flow_diagnostics.total_read: Market total stayed at 208.5 despite 95-point half. Needs 114 second-half points to clear. Model does not support chasing over at this number unless pace spikes early Q3.
flow_diagnostics.team_total_read: OKC needs 57 second-half points to clear 100.5. This is plausible with SGA normalization but -143 is heavy vig. SAS needs 52 to clear 102.5, but their scoring profile is more possession/rebound based than creation based.

liveflow_decision.lean_1: OKC TT Over 100.5 is the best raw probability angle, but -143 is not a clean value strike.
liveflow_decision.lean_2: SAS TT Over 102.5 has modest probability but is fragile due to Fox absence and turnover risk.
avoid: Full game Over 208.5 at +173 because the plus price is tempting but needs a major 2H tempo/FT spike
OKC spread chase unless SGA scoring activation appears early Q3

trigger_rules.OKC_TT_strike: Enter only if OKC TT drops to 99.5/100.5 at -125 or better, or if SGA scores 5+ points in first 4 minutes of Q3 without line jumping above 104.5.
trigger_rules.full_game_under_strike: Only if live total floats back to 211.5+ with no pace spike.
trigger_rules.SAS_side_hold: SAS live ML/plus spread only if Wemby remains on floor and Spurs continue +6 or better rebound margin after first Q3 rotation.
